original_question:
Given a natural number \( x = 2^n - 32 \), where \( n \) is a natural number. It is known that \( x \) has exactly three distinct prime divisors, one of which is equal to \( \% \). Find \( x \).

Present the answer in LaTex format: \boxed{Your answer}
